# ElizaOS Weekly Newsletter (2026-05-31 → 2026-06-06)

## 1) Executive Summary

This week was all about **shipping Cloud V2 maturity**, **tightening reliability across the agent runtime**, and **making the orchestrator experience feel more “real product” than “dev tool.”** Three highlights:

1. **Cloud V2 reached a major sync milestone (develop → main promotion).** PR **#8116** promoted `develop` to `main`, triggering production deploy workflows across the backend, frontend, provisioning worker, and agent images—bringing recent Cloud V2 stack changes fully into prod.

2. **Agents got more autonomous and more predictable.** We saw meaningful progress in how agents plan and execute tasks: improved routing (coding vs. web), keyless web fetch capability for non-Anthropic models, and multiple fixes to prevent user-facing “tool envelope” leaks.

3. **The orchestrator/workbench UX continued its rapid climb.** The Claude/Codex-style workbench work (streaming + diffs + stop) is in, and the next-generation “Odysseus-style” UI is actively being built and reviewed—setting the stage for a more premium multi-agent operator experience.

---

## 2) Development Updates (Core + Cloud + Desktop)

### Cloud & Infrastructure
- **Production sync + deploy automation:**  
  - **#8116** (merged) promoted `develop → main` to align production with Cloud V2 changes and auto-triggered production deploy workflows.
- **Native agent sandbox stability:**  
  - **#8104** (merged) fixed Cloud agent sandbox startup/routing issues and added broader test coverage around local routing and autoscaling defaults.
- **Staging auth & magic-link fixes (Steward tenant isolation):**  
  - **#8176** (merged) isolated the Steward tenant so staging magic links don’t redirect to production.  
  - Follow-on hardening: **#8179**, **#8194**, **#8196**, **#8197** (merged) addressed tenant pinning, callback flow gaps, CORS, and CSP `connect-src` for staging domains.
- **Reasoning-model billing correctness:**  
  - **#8189** (merged) ensured a minimum response-token budget for reasoning models to prevent “billed but empty” completions when `max_tokens` is too small.

### Agent Runtime & Orchestration
- **Keyless live web retrieval across models:**  
  - **#8097** (merged) introduced a **keyless `WEB_FETCH` action** so non-Anthropic models can still answer live-info prompts inline.
- **Orchestrator workbench improvements:**  
  - **#8095** (merged) delivered a **Claude-Code-style coding workbench** experience (streaming, diffs, stop controls, SSE).
- **User-facing transcript cleanliness:**  
  - **#8159** (merged) suppressed leaked `TASKS` spawn-arg JSON envelopes that some weaker planners would accidentally paste into replies.
  - **#8160** (merged) fixed routing so coding requests that mention “market/live” terms go to **coding** rather than being misclassified as web searches.
- **Error reporting & reliability fixes:**  
  - **#8098** (merged) preserved JSON-RPC `error.data` details on ACP failures—critical for debugging schema/param issues.
  - **#8161** (merged) closed a completion de-dup TOCTOU race that could cause duplicate replies.

### Desktop & Local Inference
- **Faster startup and better “first minute” experience:**  
  - **#8148** (merged) delivered **tray-first + decoupled fast startup** (opt-in).  
  - **#8151** (merged) added background warmup for embedding and post-ready warmup for voice models.
- **Local inference robustness:**  
  - **#8191** (merged) improved error signaling when llama.cpp handlers are unavailable (typed unavailability instead of generic errors).
- **Vault resilience:**  
  - **#8092** (merged) added self-healing for stale PGlite locks so unclean shutdowns don’t brick startup.

---

## 3) Community Spotlight (Discord Highlights)

Even with a quieter technical chat volume, a few threads stood out for ecosystem direction and security posture:

- **Security & plugin craftsmanship (marsel_51932):**  
  Marsel introduced themselves as an AI Engineer/Security Researcher offering **custom production-ready plugins**, **multi-agent setups**, and **agentic/on-chain security auditing**—with a strong emphasis on **execution boundary audits** to prevent “agent draining” and malicious transaction signing, plus **prompt injection defenses**. This is exactly the kind of expertise the ecosystem benefits from as agents become more financially capable.

- **DeFi integration momentum: Kamino plugin proposal:**  
  In discussion on June 2, **n4vnt** proposed building a comprehensive **Kamino (Solana) plugin** covering lending/borrowing, vaults, yield, and limit orders—closing a major gap for agents that need to *do* DeFi, not just *describe* it. **stan0473** clarified process: **all plugins now live in `elizaos/eliza`**, and contributors should fork and target the `develop` branch (with frequent rebasing due to fast-moving core changes).

- **Ecosystem opportunities & moderation:**  
  - **Intuition Box (saulodigital)** flagged upcoming bounties and asked how to share them with the ElizaOS dev community.  
  - **om1d_sa** issued a scammer alert in `#coders`, acknowledged by **odilitime**—a reminder to keep security hygiene high as the community grows.  
  - In partners, **DEMIAN (DAPPCRAFT)** reached out about a potential collaboration with an AI DePIN project (noted as ~0.5B FDV) and requested a call with Shaw.

- **Org clarity & new ideas:**  
  June 3 discussion clarified that **Hyperia is separate from ElizaOS**, and **Hyperforge** is Hyperia’s world-building component. Also spotted: **Neverbell**, an early-stage concept exploring agents interacting with financial systems through user-defined controls/permissions.

---

## 4) Token Economics (AI16z & auto.fun)

No official, source-linked updates on **AI16z token economics** or **auto.fun** shipped in the provided activity this week.

What *did* move meaningfully in the “economic substrate” direction is **platform capability**: billing and spend tracking improvements in the stack (not token-specific) continue to mature the foundation for sustainable agent hosting and monetization.

If there are token or auto.fun announcements, the best place to surface them in future newsletters is an official Discord announcement thread or a pinned forum post—so we can link canonical sources.

---

## 5) Coming Soon

A few items to keep an eye on (and/or help review):

- **Odysseus-style orchestrator UI (in review):** PR **#8180** proposes a full-window `/odysseus` workbench experience with a richer shell, panels, window manager, and `fullBleed` UI mounting support.
- **Continuous chat overlay + restored app shell (draft/in progress):** PR **#8178** explores a “Her-style” overlay that becomes the unified chat input across views (with Storybook expansion).
- **Orchestrator narration “deliverable drop” defect:** Issue **#8162** is open and well-documented—focused on ensuring sub-agent deliverables (stdout/tool output) don’t get stripped/truncated during narration.
- **Kamino plugin build-out:** the community signaled strong demand; contributors with Solana/DeFi experience can help shape safe abstractions for risk-sensitive operations.

---

## 6) Resources (Links Worth Your Time)

### Key PRs / Releases
- Cloud V2 production sync: **#8116** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8116  
- Agentic views surface + sleep/wake + incremental backups: **#8080** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8080  
- Orchestrator coding workbench (streaming/diffs/stop): **#8095** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8095  
- Keyless `WEB_FETCH` action: **#8097** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8097  
- Reasoning-model response-token budget fix: **#8189** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8189  
- Odysseus-style orchestrator UI (open): **#8180** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8180  

### Notable Issue
- Orchestrator deliverable narration dropping/garbling: **#8162** — https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8162  

### Discord Threads (source)
- June 4 community introductions & security services: https://discord.com/channels/1253563208833433701/1253563209462448241  
- June 4 coders scammer alert: https://discord.com/channels/1253563208833433701/1300025221834739744  
- June 4 partners outreach: https://discord.com/channels/1253563208833433701/1301363808421543988

# elizaOS Discord - 2026-06-02

## Summary

### ElizaOS Ecosystem Architecture

stan0473 provided a comprehensive breakdown of the ElizaOS ecosystem components to clarify the architecture. ElizaOS serves as the core agent-building framework. ElizaCloud functions as the hosting infrastructure for fully-featured agents. Milady is positioned as a consumer-facing UI for life management. Waifu is described as a side project focused on agent competition and economies, with all agents running on ElizaCloud infrastructure.

### Kamino Plugin Development

n4vnt proposed building a comprehensive Kamino plugin for ElizaOS to address a significant integration gap. Kamino is identified as a critical Solana protocol covering lending, borrowing, automated liquidity vaults, yield farms, and limit orders. The plugin aims to cover all core Kamino features, with n4vnt noting that Kamino is exploring AI adoption. anton_0413 validated the importance of this plugin, explaining it would enable ElizaOS agents to perform meaningful DeFi actions beyond simple information retrieval. The technical challenge involves designing reliable abstractions for lending, vault management, and risk-sensitive operations while maintaining developer experience simplicity. n4vnt confirmed taking a measured approach, having written only a few actions so far to ensure quality.

### Plugin Development Process

stan0473 confirmed that all plugins now live in the elizaOS/eliza repository and directed n4vnt to fork and target the develop branch. A warning was issued that development moves quickly and requires frequent rebasing to stay current with the main codebase.

## FAQ

**Q: What are the different components of the ElizaOS ecosystem?**
A: ElizaOS is the agent-building framework, ElizaCloud is the hosting infrastructure for fully-featured agents, Milady is a consumer-facing UI for life management, and Waifu is a side project for agent competition and economies where all agents run on ElizaCloud.

**Q: What is Kamino and why is a plugin needed?**
A: Kamino is a critical Solana protocol covering lending, borrowing, automated liquidity vaults, yield farms, and limit orders. A plugin is needed to enable ElizaOS agents to perform meaningful DeFi actions beyond information retrieval.

**Q: Where should new plugins be developed?**
A: All plugins now live in the elizaOS/eliza repository. Developers should fork and target the develop branch, with frequent rebasing required due to rapid development pace.

**Q: Is Waifu similar to Virtuals?**
A: This question was raised in the discussion but not explicitly answered in the provided summary.

## Help Interactions

**Helper:** stan0473
**Helpee:** n4vnt
**Resolution:** Provided guidance on plugin development process, confirming that all plugins live in elizaOS/eliza and directing to fork and target the develop branch with warnings about frequent rebasing requirements.

**Helper:** anton_0413
**Helpee:** n4vnt
**Resolution:** Validated the importance of the Kamino plugin proposal and provided context on the technical challenges involved in designing reliable abstractions for DeFi operations while maintaining simplicity.

## Action Items

### Technical

- Build comprehensive Kamino plugin covering lending, borrowing, automated liquidity vaults, yield farms, and limit orders (mentioned by n4vnt)
- Fork elizaOS/eliza repository and target develop branch for plugin development (mentioned by stan0473)
- Design reliable abstractions for lending, vault management, and risk-sensitive operations while maintaining developer experience simplicity (mentioned by anton_0413)
- Maintain frequent rebasing due to rapid development pace (mentioned by stan0473)

### Features

- Implement all core Kamino features in the plugin to enable meaningful DeFi actions for ElizaOS agents (mentioned by n4vnt and anton_0413)

# Overall Project Weekly Summary (May 31 - 6, 2026)

## Executive Summary
This week, the ElizaOS project focused on maturing our cloud infrastructure and enhancing the reliability of our autonomous AI agents. By improving how agents are billed, managed, and tasked, we have taken a significant step toward a more stable and professional-grade framework for both Web2 and Web3 users.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Cloud Infrastructure and Reliability**
_Goal: We aimed to create a more professional, scalable environment for hosting AI agents._
- We introduced automated billing and usage tracking in [elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza), allowing for clearer management of agent costs.
- We improved agent security and stability by isolating them into dedicated, private containers ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

**Enhancing Agent Intelligence and Autonomy**
_Goal: We wanted our agents to be more effective at completing complex tasks without needing constant guidance._
- We launched a new coding workbench in [elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza) that allows agents to write and edit code more efficiently.
- We refined the "brain" of our agents to better prioritize important tasks, such as coding, over simple web searches ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

**Improving the User and Developer Experience**
_Goal: We focused on making the framework faster to start and easier for new developers to learn._
- We optimized the desktop application in [elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza) to launch faster by preparing background tasks more efficiently.
- We expanded our documentation and added helpful prompts to the chat interface to make it easier for new users to get started ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- Implemented metered billing ([elizaos/eliza#8094](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8094)) and per-inference spend tracking ([elizaos/eliza#8089](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8089)) to support a mature cloud-hosted model.
- Isolated agents into dedicated containers via the sandbox registry ([elizaos/eliza#8073](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8073)).
- Launched a Claude-Code-style workbench to support streaming and code diffs ([elizaos/eliza#8095](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8095)).
- Optimized desktop startup performance through deferred model warmup ([elizaos/eliza#8151](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8151), [elizaos/eliza#8148](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8148)).
- Expanded Storybook documentation and added prompt suggestions to the chat overlay to improve onboarding ([elizaos/eliza#8200](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8200), [elizaos/eliza#8201](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8201)).
- Resolved critical build failures and agent booting regressions ([elizaos/eliza#8099](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8099), [elizaos/eliza#8100](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8100)).
- Fixed JSON-RPC error reporting and logic issues that previously hindered sub-agent performance ([elizaos/eliza#8101](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8101), [elizaos/eliza#8127](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8127)).

# Overall Project Monthly Summary (May 2026)

## Executive Summary
May 2026 was a month of foundational hardening for ElizaOS, focused on stabilizing our core framework and ensuring seamless compatibility across diverse computing environments. By resolving critical infrastructure bottlenecks and standardizing how our plugins interact with the core system, we have significantly improved the reliability and security of our AI agent deployments.

### Key Strategic Initiatives & Outcomes

**Strengthening Core Reliability and Cross-Platform Stability**
*Goal: To ensure our agents run consistently and securely on any server or operating system.*
- We hardened the agent runtime for headless environments, preventing system crashes on Linux and Windows servers ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We implemented a new, secure way to manage sensitive information across different platforms, making it easier to integrate with our settings interface ([elizaos/eliza](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza)).
- We resolved complex system-level errors that previously hindered reliable agent performance in headless deployments ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow)).

**Modernizing Our Build Architecture**
*Goal: To align our plugin ecosystem with modern web standards for better performance and easier maintenance.*
- We transitioned multiple plugins ([plugin-ollama](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-ollama), [plugin-openrouter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openrouter), [plugin-pdf](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-pdf), and [plugin-openai](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-openai)) to a modern, modular build structure, eliminating runtime conflicts with the core framework.

**Ensuring Secure and Reliable Integrations**
*Goal: To maintain high security and service continuity for our decentralized exchange and communication tools.*
- We migrated our Jupiter integration to the official API endpoint and implemented mandatory security key authentication to keep our decentralized trading features compliant and reliable ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter)).
- We refined configuration management for Telegram and Discord plugins to ensure that agent settings are applied correctly and consistently during runtime ([elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram), [elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord)).

### Cross-Repository Coordination
- **Shared Framework Alignment**: A major project-wide effort was undertaken to align all plugins with the ESM-first architecture of the core framework. By removing legacy code formats across multiple repositories, we ensured that the entire ElizaOS ecosystem remains compatible, modular, and free of runtime errors.
- **Unified Runtime Fixes**: Coordination between the core framework and the n8n-workflow plugin allowed us to resolve deep-seated issues regarding authentication and message handling, ensuring that user settings propagate correctly from the dashboard to the agent runtime.

## Repository Spotlights

### elizaos/eliza
- Hardened the runtime for headless servers by bypassing unavailable system secret services ([#7230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7230)).
- Introduced `@elizaos/vault` to enable secure, cross-platform secrets management ([#7197](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7197)).
- Fixed critical authentication regressions, including SIWE failures and bot token bridging ([#7288](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7288), [#7327](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7327)).
- Improved user interaction by adding clarification workflows for n8n and better context integration for Discord ([#7316](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7316), [#7315](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7315)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow
- Formalized how agents request missing information from users by implementing `ClarificationRequest` structures ([#27](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/27)).
- Improved prompt routing reliability by switching to ID-based directives ([#28](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/28)).
- Resolved critical runtime crashes and message loss issues that affected Telegram bot polling ([#7244](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7244), [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/7245)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter
- Successfully migrated to the official `api.jup.ag` endpoint to ensure service continuity ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).
- Implemented mandatory API key authentication to meet modern security standards for swap requests ([#4](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-jupiter/pull/4)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord
- Addressed configuration inconsistencies where voice modules bypassed standard auto-reply settings ([#49](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/issues/49), [#50](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/50)).

### elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram
- Fixed a configuration issue to ensure Telegram setup tokens are correctly applied during runtime execution ([#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-telegram/pull/29)).

      "body": "## Summary\n\nWhen the planner narrates a coding sub-agent's `task_complete` back to the user, the **deliverable** (the printed value / command output) is frequently dropped, truncated, or buried under process chatter — while the underlying sub-agent work is correct. This is a *presentation* defect, not a capability one.\n\nEvidence: a live 48-prompt sweep (30-scenario battery + smart/normie/harden suites) across all three backends found **0 capability-broken defects** — every answer was correct, every spawn produced its artifact, every safety probe handled. The **only** remaining defect class is this narration-envelope.\n\n## Manifestations (all observed live)\n\n1. **Deliverable drop on combo / printed-value tasks.** On the no-change-set completion path, `composeNarration` → `stripToolTranscript(response)` (`sub-agent-router.ts`) deletes the entire `[tool output: …] … [/tool output]` block. For a \"print a value / run a command and report the output\" task (no files changed → no diff), that block *is* the deliverable, so it's gone before the user sees it.\n2. **Value truncation by the parent model.** The synthetic `task_complete` memory is re-rendered by the parent `RESPONSE_HANDLER` (a weak planner in some deployments), which truncates the value — e.g. `2026-06-02` → `202`, a BTC price `70234` → `705`. Per-backend signature: opencode truncates, codex emits verbose process narration + `Completed <task>` banners, claude is cleanest.\n3. **Stale retry attempts leak.** When a first attempt errors (e.g. `session_state_lost` / an ACP crash) and a respawn recovers, the user sees *both* `\"the sub-agent was blocked / crashed, let me try again\"` **and** the eventual deliverable.\n4. **Process chatter.** Sub-agents (esp. codex) narrate their steps (`\"I'll load the workspace context first\"`, `\"rg is not installed\"`, `\"reverse.py already exists\"`); the parent relays it.\n\n## Proposed structural fixes\n\n**(a) Carry the printed-value deliverable verbatim (fixes #1, mitigates #2).** A structural carrier that mirrors the existing verified-URL carrier:\n- *Producer* (`sub-agent-router.ts`): on the `task_complete && !changeSet && verifiedUrls.length === 0` path, recover the captured stdout from the **raw** `response` (before `composeNarration` strips it) via the inverse of `stripToolTranscript`, and stamp it as `metadata.subAgentDeliverable` (gated to a short single block, ≤2KB, so multi-KB transcripts stay on the model-rendered path). *(I have a ready, tested producer patch for this.)*\n- *Consumer* (`evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts`): when `subAgentDeliverable` is present, relay it **verbatim** instead of letting the model re-summarize/truncate it — parallel to how `subAgentVerifiedUrls` is already consumed.\n\n**(b) Terminal-reply filter (fixes #3, #4).** Emit only the *terminal* sub-agent reply; suppress stale `blocked`/`failed`/`state_lost` attempts that a later retry superseded, plus raw tool chatter and `Completed <task>` banners. **Caveat — needs care:** the `session_state_lost` error event is currently *coupled to the respawn trigger* (re-injecting it is what prompts the parent to respawn), so suppressing the user-facing narration requires an internal-only routing path that keeps the respawn signal. This is the part that needs design discussion.\n\n**(c) Already shipped — source-side mitigation.** #8158 adds an output-discipline section to the scaffolded sub-agent operating manual (\"lead with the deliverable, don't narrate process, no `Completed <task>` banner, a bare workspace has no SOUL.md/USER.md — don't hunt for them\"), which measurably reduced #4 at the source (codex re-probes went from a verbose banner to a bare `5050` / `2026-06-02`).\n\n## Coordination note\n\n`sub-agent-router.ts` and `evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts` were recently rewritten on `develop`; this should be coordinated with that work rather than landed blind. Happy to open the (a)-producer + (a)-consumer as a focused PR if the approach looks right.",

      "body": "## context\n\nafter #7988 lands the windows-x86_64 ffi build path, `libeliza-llama-shim.dll` loads into `bun:ffi` with all 39 symbols resolved. the very next layer — prewarm — crashes with a ggml batch-sizing assert on `eliza-1-0_8b` text bundle.\n\n## crash\n\n```\nGGML_ASSERT(n_tokens_all <= cparams.n_batch) failed\n```\n\nrepro: `~/.eliza` already has the `eliza-1-0_8b-128k.gguf` bundle staged; on first activation the prewarm batch builder asks the runtime to decode more tokens than the context's `n_batch` slot allows. fatal.\n\n## environment\n\n- platform: windows-x86_64\n- backend: cuda (cuda 12.4, msvc, ninja, llama.cpp pinned to the ref in `build-llama-cpp-desktop-dylib.mjs`)\n- shim: built from `eliza_llama_shim.c` with the stub set added in #7988\n- bundle: `bundles/0_8b/text/eliza-1-0_8b-128k.gguf` from default-user `~/.eliza/local-inference/`\n- runtime: `desktop-llama-adapter.ts` → `eliza_llama_decode_unified` path\n\n## what i think is going on\n\nthe prewarm path appears to construct a batch that targets the manifest's declared context window (128k tokens) without first clamping against the actual `cparams.n_batch` value the ffi context was created with. on linux/macos this might be masked by larger default `n_batch` or different prewarm sizing; on windows-cuda the assert fires.\n\ncandidates (i don't want to put words in anyone's mouth):\n\n1. prewarm should size the batch off `cparams.n_batch`, not `n_ctx`.\n2. context creation on the desktop adapter should set `n_batch >= prewarm_tokens` for the chosen bundle.\n3. the bundle manifest's recommended prewarm token count should be exposed and used as both inputs.\n\n## scope\n\n- not blocked by anything outstanding once #7988 lands.\n- separate from the build-time fixes in #7988 (which are infrastructure) — this is a runtime sizing bug one layer down.\n- related context PR: #7983 (local-inference dflash discovery + runtime router wiring).\n",

Pollutes the channel.\n\n## Suggested fix\n\nUSE_SKILL action's result construction should produce a clean \\`userFacingText\\` (the actual command output) separate from the diagnostic \\`text\\` (which can include cmd + envelopes for trajectory recording).       "id": "I_kwDOMT5cIs8AAAABEDBuaA",
      "title": "orchestrator: served-200 build false-flagged as stale by mtime freshness gate (GAP-C retry/recall regression)",
      "author": "NubsCarson",
      "number": 8127,
      "repository": "elizaos/eliza",
      "body": "### Summary\n\n`verifyLocalTarget` in `plugins/plugin-agent-orchestrator/src/services/sub-agent-router.ts` false-positives on a healthy, served static app, marking it \"dead\" purely because its local file mtime is older than `session.createdAt - 5s`.\n\n### Root cause\n\nIn the completion-URL verification pass (`annotateUnverifiedUrls`), after a URL probes a healthy HTTP 200 (`probe()` returns `status === null`), the code still consults `verifyMappedLocalUrl` → `verifyLocalTarget`, which applies a wall-clock freshness gate:\n\n```ts\nif (requireFresh && stat.mtimeMs < sessionStartedAtMs - 5000) {\n  return `mapped local target was not updated during this session: ...`;\n}\n```\n\nA static app (e.g. `color-pop/index.html`) that exists, is non-empty, and serves HTTP 200 gets flagged \"dead\" because the deploy step copied the build into place and preserved the source file's mtime (mtime skew vs `session.createdAt`).\n\n### Impact (GAP-C chain)\n\n1. The false-dead triggers a spurious verify-retry.\n2. The retry suppresses the round-1 `task_complete`.\n3. The in-flight retry session trips `newerTaskSince` in `coding-session-changes.ts`.\n4. The REAL diff is withheld — so a follow-up \"what did you change?\" wrongly answers \"no captured file diff\".\n\n### Fix\n\nThe live HTTP 200 probe is authoritative for a served URL. The mtime-freshness comparison must not override a healthy 200; reserve the freshness gate for cases where the URL is NOT serving (where the local file is the only liveness signal). The missing-file and empty-file checks remain unconditional.\n\nTracked by PR with a regression test that pins both the served-200 (not flagged) and unserved (still flagged) cases.",

      "body": "## Summary\n\nAdds a full **chat-shell UI for the multi-agent orchestrator**, modeled on the\n[odysseus](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus) frontend (MIT) and\nrebuilt from scratch in **React on `@elizaos/ui`**, wired to the **existing\nelizaOS runtime**. This is **frontend only** — there is no new backend; all\ntask/session state continues to live in `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.\n\nThe new surface renders at **`/odysseus`** (and the existing **`/orchestrator`**\nroute) as a full-window app: a labeled session sidebar, a rich composer, a theme\nengine, the memory/skills/notes/settings panels, and the full set of tool views.\n\n> Credit: the original UI/UX design is from **odysseus** (MIT). This is an\n> independent React reimplementation on elizaOS's own stack — none of the\n> odysseus Python backend is used.\n\n## What's included\n\n**New `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/odysseus/` shell (~40 files):**\n- `OdysseusShell` root + `SessionSidebar` (folders, multi-select, pin, drag,\n  resize) + collapsible 48px `IconRail` (mutually exclusive with the sidebar)\n- `Composer` with slash-command + emoji autocomplete and a bottom action row\n- Theme engine (`ThemeMenu` / `PresetsPanel`): preset recolor + customize\n  (font / density / background), mapped onto elizaOS semantic CSS vars\n- `MemoryPanel` (memories/skills/add/settings), `SkillsPanel`, `NotesPanel`,\n  `SettingsPanel`\n- Tool views: tasks, models, email, calendar, gallery (+ editor), compare,\n  research, voice, group chat, cookbook, document library, admin\n- A cross-view **window manager** (`WindowManager` + `useWindowControls`):\n  drag / resize / edge-snap / minimize-to-dock (`MinimizedDock`)\n- `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useEscapeClose`, `useTaskRoom`, `useChatSubmit`\n\n**Orchestrator render helpers:**\n- `orchestrator-markdown` (via `marked`), `orchestrator-plan`,\n  `orchestrator-reasoning`\n- `orchestrator-stream` now always renders the user's own `stdin` messages\n  (preserves the existing `senderKind === \"user\"` fix)\n\n**App-shell `fullBleed` capability (`packages/ui`):**\n- Opt-in `fullBleed?: boolean` on `AppShellPageRegistration`\n  (backward-compatible). When set, the host shell mounts the page edge-to-edge\n  via `ViewRouter` — **no header, no tab bar, no routed padding** — so a view\n  that owns its whole window has no surrounding dashboard chrome.\n- This **replaces the prior hardcoded `tab !== \"orchestrator\"` header\n  special-case** in `RoutedShellContent` with a general, declarative flag.\n- `/orchestrator` and the new `/odysseus` route are registered `fullBleed: true`.\n\n**Other:**\n- adds the `marked` dependency to the plugin\n- isolates each `*.live.e2e.test.ts` suite's default chromium profile dir so\n  parallel vitest file execution can't collide on the ProcessSingleton lock\n\n## Verification\n\nBuilt on a fresh branch off current `develop` and verified there:\n\n- biome check — clean across all 53 changed source files\n- `tsgo --noEmit` (plugin) — **0 errors** against current `develop` APIs\n- `tsgo --noEmit` (`@elizaos/ui`) — 0 errors in `App.tsx` / `app-shell-registry.ts`\n- plugin unit tests — **19/19** pass\n- plugin build — `tsup` + views bundle + type declarations all succeed\n- live e2e (`*.live.e2e.test.ts`) — **26/26** pass against a running dev stack,\n  and Playwright render checks confirm the host header is absent on `/odysseus`\n  (full-bleed) and the global chat overlay behaves correctly\n\n## Scope / notes\n\n- **Frontend only.** No actions/providers/services added; state stays in\n  `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.\n- The `fullBleed` flag is opt-in and changes no existing page's behavior.\n- Some per-view actions render honest empty/disabled states where elizaOS has no\n  corresponding client method yet (e.g. email IMAP/SMTP, calendar CalDAV,\n  gallery image backend, voice TTS/STT) — these light up unchanged once the\n  matching runtime routes exist. Happy to file follow-up issues for these.\n\nScreenshots / a short screen recording can be added on request.\n\n## Related\n\n- Closes #8188 (proposal/motivation).\n- Unblocked by #8187 — a develop-side `biome` format fix for `plugin-app-control/src/views/ViewManagerView.tsx` that currently red-fails the repo-wide **Format Check** gate (and every open PR). This PR's Format Check goes green once #8187 lands.\n\n## CI status (for reviewers)\n\nThis PR's own gates are green: Format Check (this PR's files), Server Tests, unit-tests, lint-and-types, integration-tests, coverage on changed files, gitleaks, check-pr-title, route-coverage, iOS Simulator Build, Homepage Build, Electrobun Desktop Contract, Cloud Live E2E.\n\nThe currently-red lanes are **pre-existing on `develop`**, not introduced here — proven by #8187 (a 1-file format-only change) failing the *identical* set:\n\n- Plugin Tests — `@elizaos/plugin-rlm` (`server.test.ts` spawns `/nonexistent/python`)\n- Client Tests — `FineTuningTuiView.test.tsx` (plugin-training)\n- Zero-Key Deterministic E2E, `bun run dev onboarding chat`, `env:audit:check`\n- Android Debug Build — `shortcuts.xml` deep-link audit (`run-mobile-build.mjs`)\n- Format Check (biome) — develop's `ViewManagerView.tsx` (→ #8187)\n\nA short screen recording / screenshots of the live `/odysseus` surfaces can be added on request.\n",
